# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment for our batch processing jobs. The pods run data processing tasks and don't need to interact with the Kubernetes API. Use default service account settings for simplicity. Focus on getting the jobs running reliably.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1beta2  # use beta version of APIs if you have available or create a new namespace (e.g., kubectl get ns) first and then apply below command in that specific NS, ex : `kubectl --namespace=<your_ns> apply -f <this_file>`
kind: Deployment  # Kubernetes resource kind is 'Deployment'
metadata:  
 name: batch-processing  # define your deployment name here. It will be used as the label of k8s object and also service for accessing it (e.g., get pod/job names using `kubectl --namespace=<your_ns> get deployments`)
 namespace: default   # specify in which 'default' or any existing Kubernetes Namespace your Deployment is to be created, by setting a value here and make sure it aligns with the NS you defined above. If not specified then same as current context ie., kubectl config view | grep Current 
spec:   # define specifications of 'Deployment' object which contains podTemplateSpec details (i.e container definitions) about how to run your tasks inside Pod(s).   
 replicas: 2     # specify the number instances for Deploying in a Kubernetes cluster, if you need more or less set here as per requirement  
 selector:       # define criteria which pod should be selected by k8s controller and where it can run. In other words 'match label selectors' of Pod(s) are applied to the container for that Deployment  . This is how Kubernetes knows what components do we need on our server (i.e., in this case data processing tasks).
   matchLabels:    # define labels which should be used by selector criteria i.e pod name as a unique identifier, then it can know to run the container of that label with `kubectl get po -n default` command for more information about 'pod', or  you may use same one here if already set in deployment and need any changes
     app: batch-processing   # identify your pods by their labels, usually they are given using name field i.e., "app=myApp" to define which group of Pod(s) have this particular label defined (i.e,"Label Selectors for running tasks in K8S") 
 template:      # details about the container that will be run on each pod, it can contain information like how many replicas are created here and also where image is located etc., It'll use default service account settings which allow access to local filesystem if not specified otherwise. If 'imagePullPolicy', defaults as Always 
   metadata:     # Details about the container that will be run on each pod, it can contain information like how many replicas are created here and also where image is located etc., It'll use default service account settings which allow access to local filesystem if not specified otherwise. If 'imagePullPolicy', defaults as Always
    labels:       # Details about the container that will be run on each pod, it can contain information like how many replicas are created here and also where image is located etc., It'll use default service account settings which allow access to local filesystem if not specified otherwise. If 'imagePullPolicy', defaults as Always
      app: batch-processing # specify the label for identifying this pod  (i,e,"app=myApp") - we can match all Pods under that labeled group with `kubectl get po --namespace default` command and see labels of each ones. If you have defined your own tags then make sure they are same as below here if not it will be a new tag
    spec:  # define specification details about how to create container(i,e., tasks inside Pods) for running in K8S cluster   which includes what image is used (which can also pull from local or remote repository etc.), environment variables passed when pod starts up and ports exposed if needed. Here it'll be by default settings like 'imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent', container restart policy, ‘tty’ enabled during start of a process inside the Container et al
      containers:    # details about each individual instance (container) created in Pods or instances which can run tasks. For example if you want to have multiple batch processing jobs and one runs on CPU while others use GPU/CPU, this information will be provided here by name along with its required resources as defined above under 'resources'
        -   # define details about a container instance (i ib the case of job-1), which can run tasks for data processed inside Pods. Here it contains ‘name’ and how many replicas to create this particular task from scratch when K8S controller starts up, what image should be used in Container et al
          name: batchJob   # specify a unique identifier ie., 'batchjob' which can uniquely identify the container instance created by kubernetes for running tasks (i.e,'running data processing job') - see using command like `kubectl get pods` or similar commands and you will find related information about each one
          image: myimage   # specify name of Docker Image which can run in the Pod as per requirement, if not provided default docker images are used by k8s controller.  'my-project/batchprocessing' should be replaced with actual details regarding your project and batch processing jobs e.g., "registry.example.com/my_user/job:latest"
          resources:   # define how much CPU or memory (as requested) a container is allowed to use if required by tasks which it runs inside Pods  - should be defined in terms of the quantity and unit like '250m' for half-a second, also depends on your requirements. If not provided defaults will take place as per requirement
            limits:   # define upper bounds (how much resources to limit) if required by tasks which it runs inside Pods  - should be defined in terms of the quantity and unit like '250m' for half-a second, also depends on your requirements. If not provided defaults will take place as per requirement
              cpu: "1"   # define upper bound (how much resources to limit) if required by tasks which it runs inside Pods  - should be defined in terms of the quantity and unit like '250m' for half-a second, also depends on your requirements. If not provided defaults will take place as per requirement
              memory: "1Gi"   # define upper bound (how much resources to limit) if required by tasks which it runs inside Pods  - should be defined in terms of the quantity and unit like '250m' for half-a second, also depends on your requirements. If not provided defaults will take place as per requirement
